data.title: 3-Day Penang  Weekend

stops.description: Classic dim sum pushcart experience in Georgetown

stops.description: Legendary roti canai since the 1950s — widely considered the gold standard in Penang. The perfectly flaky, crisp-on-the-outside, soft-on-the-inside roti is served with rich, aromatic dhal curry and fiery sambal. Locals line up before sunrise for this iconic breakfast.
stops.aiReasoning: Selected for your breakfast — a hidden gem locals love
stops.address: 114, 110, Jalan Transfer, George Town, 10050 George Town, Pulau Pinang, Malaysia

stops.description: Coastal hike to secluded Monkey Beach and lighthouse

stops.description: Instagram-worthy coffee bar with exotic beans

hotelOptions.name: Eastern & Oriental Hotel
hotelOptions.description: Grand colonial heritage hotel on the seafront — a Penang icon since 1885 with stunning ocean views, marble bathrooms, and legendary afternoon tea.

hotelOptions.name: Shangri-La Rasa Sayang
hotelOptions.description: 5-star resort on Batu Ferringhi beach with sprawling tropical gardens, championship golf course, and world-renowned CHI Spa.

hotelOptions.name: G Hotel Kelawai
hotelOptions.description: Contemporary luxury hotel on Gurney Drive with stylish interiors, rooftop infinity pool, and panoramic sea views.

hotelOptions.name: Muntri Mews
hotelOptions.description: Boutique heritage hotel in a restored 19th-century shophouse — each room uniquely designed with antique furnishings and local art.

hotelOptions.name: JEN Penang Georgetown
hotelOptions.description: Modern hotel in the heart of Georgetown's UNESCO zone — walking distance to street art, cafes, and clan jetties.

hotelOptions.name: Cheong Fatt Tze Blue Mansion
hotelOptions.description: UNESCO award-winning heritage boutique hotel in an iconic indigo courtyard mansion — featured in Crazy Rich Asians.

hotelOptions.name: Macalister Mansion
hotelOptions.description: Design-forward boutique hotel in a restored colonial mansion with art installations, French-inspired dining, and a serene pool.

hotelOptions.name: The Prestige Hotel
hotelOptions.description: Victorian-inspired design hotel with whimsical architecture, rooftop pool, and a secret garden in the lobby.

hotelOptions.name: Tido Hostel
hotelOptions.description: Stylish budget hostel with pod-style beds, rooftop terrace, and social atmosphere — perfect for solo travelers.

hotelOptions.name: Hotel Penaga
hotelOptions.description: Heritage hotel combining three restored shophouses with spacious suites, antique furniture, and a tranquil spa.

tips: Download offline Google Maps for areas with poor reception
Use MRT/LRT for city travel in KL — cheap and avoids traffic jams
Street food is where you find Malaysia's best flavors at the best prices
Try local drinks — teh tarik, cendol, nutmeg juice, calamansi lime
